definition: Fiscal expansion can simultaneously fail to boost demand (partial Ricardian offset, multiplier 0.5-0.8) while pushing long-end yields higher through term premium from supply absorption — producing unambiguously correlation-positive effects at 10Y+ maturities without corresponding demand-side growth.
evidence: Fiscal purgatory concept: multiplier sustains output without resolution
Explains divergence between short-end and long-end correlation behavior
open_questions: Whether Ricardian behavior is stable or shifts with fiscal deficit size
How to measure the Ricardian offset in real time
